page: Microsoft Azure HB368-336rs_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the HBv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 336 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors. The system is equipped with 448.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.33 GB per core. This hardware configuration is designed for high-density compute workloads that require massive parallel processing capabilities rather than large memory footprints per thread.

For storage-heavy operations, the instance includes 15851 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations. It is also RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput inter-node communication. This makes it suitable for multi-node clustering and tightly coupled parallel computing tasks. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration.

From a resource density perspective, the HB368-336rs_v5 offers high compute density and substantial local storage capacity. However, the low memory allocation of 1.33 GB per core presents a tradeoff, as memory-bound applications or large-scale in-memory databases may experience performance limitations. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is optimized for workloads that can fully utilize the massive core count and RDMA capabilities without requiring high memory-per-core ratios.

This instance is designed for high-performance computing (HPC) workloads, large-scale parallel simulations, computational fluid dynamics, weather modeling, and distributed storage tasks that benefit from dedicated vCPUs, RDMA networking, and high local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure HB368-336rs_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server within the HBv5 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 336 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel processors and is paired with 448.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 1.33 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 15851 GB of local storage, supports Premium Storage, and is RDMA capable for low-latency networking. It does not include any GPUs. This configuration is engineered for high-density parallel compute tasks rather than memory-intensive applications, presenting a clear tradeoff between massive processing power and a low memory-per-core ratio. It is suited for high-performance computing (HPC) workloads, large-scale simulations, and distributed storage applications.

tagline: A high-density compute server featuring three hundred thirty-six dedicated cores, massive local storage, and low-latency RDMA networking for parallel workloads.
